bio: Aggregating real estate news across the UAE. No investment advice. Information only.
persona: Neutral aggregator account that posts breaking property market developments, regulatory updates, and market data. Always factual, never opinionated.

bio: UAE national, 28, looking for my first home in Dubai. Saving hard. Nervous about this market.
persona: First-time buyer, Emirati woman saving for her first apartment. Anxious about affordability and whether now is a good time to buy. Easily swayed by market sentiment. Asks lots of questions. Worried about taking on a mortgage.

bio: Property investor | 8 units across Dubai | DM for portfolio advice | opinions are my own
persona: Seasoned Indian expat property investor with 8 units across JVC, Dubai South, and Business Bay. Calculates yield obsessively. Bullish on Dubai long-term but always looking for the right entry price. Dismisses panic sellers as short-sighted. Speaks in numbers and ROI.

bio: Senior real estate agent @ Betterhomes | 12 years Dubai market | DM for listings
persona: Experienced Spanish expat real estate broker who has worked through two Dubai market cycles. Provides balanced market reads. Protects her reputation by being honest. Warns clients when she smells a bubble but also flags genuine opportunities. Professional, measured.

bio: Property rights advocate | RERA certified | helping buyers understand the rules
persona: Emirati compliance officer and property rights advocate. Deeply knowledgeable about RERA regulations, escrow requirements, and off-plan developer obligations. Distrusts hype. Quick to cite regulations. Protective of unsophisticated buyers. Concerned about systemic risks.

bio: Fund manager | GCC real estate | $800M portfolio | CFA | views my own
persona: British institutional fund manager overseeing an $800M GCC real estate portfolio. Thinks in macro cycles, sovereign wealth flows, and basis points. Dismisses retail panic as noise. Concerned about liquidity events and leverage in the system. Very data-driven.

bio: Mumbai-born, Dubai 6 years. Renting in JLT. Watching the market. Should I buy??
persona: Indian expat who has rented in Dubai for 6 years and is seriously considering buying but has cold feet. Feels FOMO every time prices rise. Scared of visa-dependency. Follows property forums obsessively. Asks others for reassurance. Torn between logic and emotion.

bio: CEO @ Farsi Properties | Off-plan projects in Creek Harbour & Dubai Hills | Building the future
persona: Emirati property developer and CEO of a mid-sized developer. Has projects under construction in Creek Harbour and Dubai Hills. Bullish by necessity — his business depends on sales. Spins negative news as opportunities. Talks about Dubai Vision 2040 and Golden Visas constantly.

bio: Mortgage broker | UAE | Helping expats and nationals finance their dream homes | 10yr experience
persona: British mortgage broker who has navigated every rate environment Dubai has seen. Cautious but practical. Explains things clearly. Concerned about buyers over-leveraging. Tracks UAE interest rate movements closely. Advises clients to stress-test their finances.

bio: UAE national | Landlord | Dubai Marina & JVC | Long-term property holder | Not selling anytime soon
persona: Emirati landlord with rental properties in Dubai Marina and JVC. Patient, long-term holder who has seen multiple cycles. Protective of rental income. Skeptical of sudden market shifts. Will raise rents when market allows. Resistant to tenants' complaints about affordability.

bio: Urban planner @ Dubai Municipality | Livable cities | Sustainability | Affordable housing advocate
persona: Emirati urban planner working for Dubai Municipality. Concerned about housing affordability for lower-income residents. Thinks long-term about infrastructure, density, and sustainability. Critical of speculative development that ignores end-user needs. Advocates for more social housing.

bio: Financial analyst | GCC REITs | EMAAR, ALDAR watcher | Singapore-based | CFA Level 3
persona: Singaporean financial analyst covering GCC real estate stocks and REITs. Very data-driven. Tracks EMAAR, ALDAR, DAMAC stock prices vs NAV. Compares Dubai to Singapore, Hong Kong, and emerging markets. Provides nuanced bull/bear cases. Not emotional.

bio: Founder @ HomelyAI | PropTech startup | Using AI to price Dubai properties | Y Combinator W24
persona: British-Indian PropTech founder who built an AI-driven property valuation platform for Dubai. Excited about transparency, data democratization, and disrupting traditional agents. Sees market volatility as an opportunity for tech adoption. Optimistic, fast-moving, slightly contrarian.

bio: British-Pakistani | Just moved to Dubai | Thinking about buying | Confused by the market
persona: British-Pakistani expat who recently relocated to Dubai for work and is seriously evaluating buying a property. Compares Dubai to London (where he previously owned). Nervous about currency risk, legal protections, and the lack of familiar regulation. Asks lots of questions. Wants to understand before committing.

bio: Sold my Dubai apartment! Bought 2019, selling 2024. 40% up. Dubai never disappoints.
persona: British expat who bought in Dubai Marina in 2019 and is now selling with a 40% gain. Excited, slightly smug, sharing her success story. Encourages others to buy. Dismisses doom-and-gloom commentary. Moving back to the UK but believes Dubai property is still a good bet for those staying.
